targets.sourceTitle: Sample Syllabus Language: AI Course Policies
expectedThemes: sample permitted and prohibited use language

stopConditions: Stop after official UMD pages support narrow university-wide and teaching-resource claims.
Do not infer binding university-wide rules from sample syllabus language; scope those as instructor guidance.
Do not use surveys, news items, or study reports as binding policy evidence.

artifacts.queryUsed: site:umd.edu University of Maryland College Park generative AI policy artificial intelligence teaching academic integrity
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official AI @ UMD page with approved/effective GenAI guidelines for UMD community members.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Sample Syllabus Language: AI Course Policies
artifacts.queryUsed: follow TLTC Artificial Intelligence page to sample syllabus language
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official TLTC sample-language page; claims from this source are limited to instructor course-policy guidance.

methodsAttempted.query: site:umd.edu University of Maryland College Park generative AI policy artificial intelligence teaching academic integrity
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the official AI @ UMD guidelines page, TLTC academic integrity page, AI resource hub, Provost announcement, survey/report material, and non-policy/news-like results.

methodsAttempted.query: site:umd.edu UMD generative AI privacy data confidential information guidance
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the UMD guidelines page on the ai-resources host plus survey, syllabus, and research-document results.
Links from the central guidelines/resource pages led to approved services, approved software, and TLTC teaching resources.
robots.txt for ai.umd.edu allowed the selected public resources; restricted directories were not crawled.
robots.txt for tltc.umd.edu did not disallow the selected public teaching pages.

artifacts.summary: Official UMD pages provided enough direct evidence for narrow claims about university-wide GenAI guidelines, approved tools, data handling, procurement review, and instructor course-policy guidance. Survey/report/news-like sources were rejected for policy specificity.

artifacts.notes: Official UMD analytics/survey page discovered during search, but not used as policy evidence because it reports campus survey context rather than operative guidance.
Official PDF discovered during search appears to be an academic integrity AI study/report; not used for binding policy claims.
UMD-domain news-like item discovered during search, but not used because the selected official guideline and TLTC pages provide stronger policy-specific evidence.

artifacts.claimText: UMD publishes GenAI guidelines that apply to faculty, staff, students, and affiliates using GenAI tools in academic, research, or administrative activities.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For coursework, UMD tells students to assume GenAI use for assignments and assessments is not allowed unless the syllabus or assignment instructions specify otherwise.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UMD strongly encourages instructors to establish course-specific policies defining appropriate and inappropriate GenAI use.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UMD's Division of Academic Affairs advises against incorporating GenAI detection tools into course policies and says detection results should be treated only as potential indicators, not definitive proof or the sole basis for grading decisions.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UMD guidance tells researchers not to input federal, state, or UMD data into externally sourced GenAI tools and not to upload unpublished research data or other confidential information into tools that have not undergone proper review.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For administrative work, UMD guidance says administrative staff should not input non-public institutional data into externally sourced GenAI platforms and faculty or staff should not put moderate-risk Level 2 or higher data into public external GenAI platforms.

artifacts.claimText: UMD guidance says individuals intending to use UMD credentials to access or purchase products with GenAI functionality should contact the Division of IT before signing up, including for free or open-source products.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow scope claim tied directly to the Scope section.
Approved because the claim preserves the source's unless-specified course context.

artifacts.title: University of Maryland, College Park AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: Official UMD sources show a central GenAI guidelines page effective January 15, 2025, with university-wide scope across teaching, learning, research, and administrative work. The strongest evidence supports claims about course-specific instructor policies, default student expectations unless course materials permit GenAI, caution around GenAI detection tools, restrictions on sensitive data in external GenAI tools, DIT/SRM review for AI tools, and official AI services/software listings.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Sample syllabus language is instructor guidance and should not be treated as a binding university-wide student rule.
Survey, report, and news-like UMD-domain sources discovered during search were rejected for broad policy claims.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
